title: release-candidate-proof/v2
description: Evidence that a candidate tag at an exact SHA satisfies every release precondition. v2 requires the graph-witnessed required-proof/v2 document and is emitted only on success by verify-release-exact-sha.

schema.description: Contract discriminator. Consumers must reject documents whose schema they do not implement rather than best-effort parse them.

repo.description: Repository this evidence was produced in. The repo name lives in the payload, never in the schema, so the schema file stays byte-identical across repos.

candidate.description: The tag under consideration. It does not exist yet: this proof validates a candidate without creating it.

version.description: Workspace version the tree declares. Must correspond to the tag; a tag naming a different version than the tree ships is rejected (E_TAG_VERSION_MISMATCH).

required_proof.description: Reference to the graph-witnessed required-proof/v2 document backing this candidate. Its SHA must equal source.sha, so a release cannot inherit a proof from an earlier commit (E_STALE_SHA).

required_ci.description: Observed CI state for the exact candidate SHA. A required job that is non-terminal or non-success makes this proof invalid (E_REQUIRED_CI_NOT_GREEN); advisory jobs are recorded but never gate.

artifacts.description: Evidence artifacts this release stands on (e.g. the live version-matrix result). Each artifact's SHA must equal source.sha: an artifact recorded for a parent commit is a substitution, not evidence for this one (E_ARTIFACT_SHA_MISMATCH).

verdict.description: Pinned by const: this document exists only for a candidate that passed every precondition. A candidate that fails produces findings and no document.

tier.description: required: gates the release. advisory: reported separately, never gating.

status.description: Run lifecycle state. Anything other than completed is non-terminal and can never count as green.

conclusion.description: Terminal result, or null while the job is non-terminal. Only success counts as green: skipped and neutral are not success.

sha.description: The commit this artifact was recorded for.

sha1.description: Full 40-character lowercase hex git object name. Abbreviated or uppercase SHAs are rejected: evidence must name an exact, unambiguous commit.

sourceRef.description: The exact tree this evidence was produced from.

tree_clean.description: False means uncommitted changes were present. Evidence from a dirty tree is not reproducible and is rejected (E_TREE_DIRTY).
